structured_payload_candidate.title: Dynamic Glazing Tint Levels
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Dynamic architectural glazing typically corresponds to four visible light transmittance levels: 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60%.

evidence_text:
AS H R AE  G R E E N G U I D E   1 1 3
D yn a m i c  G l a zi n g
G E N E R AL D E S C R I P TI O N
Dynamic ar chitectural glazing uses electrochromic technology to
dar k en the interior of  a piece of  glass,  enabling a thin coating to change
f r om clear to tinted when small electr ical voltage is applied.  A demon-
str ation of  this technology is eyeglasses that darken when one goes out-
doors.  Dynamic glass f or architectural applications can tr ansf or m into
f our  dark ness levels d epend ing on the season,  being dar ker  in summer
to keep solar gain out and minimize glare .  In ad dition,  because the
glazing becomes d ark er  in the summer when exposed  to sun,  its use
will generally minimize r adiation heat tr ansf er and help impr ove ther -
mal comf ort in the space, .  Typically,  the tint corr espond s to 4%,  20%,
40%,  and  60% visible light transmittance T
vis  d epending on manuf ac-
turers.
judge_reason: Dynamic glazing tint levels are about architectural glazing, not chiller HVAC equipment; no operational relevance to chillers.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dynamic Glazing Application Feasibility
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Dynamic glazing is most feasible on large glazing facades not facing north, and works well on east and west orientations.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dynamic Glazing South Facade Use

structured_payload_candidate.title: Desiccant Dehumidification Application Examples
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Examples of candidates for desiccant dehumidification include facilities handling hygroscopic materials, film drying, and manufacturing of candy, chocolate, drugs, chemicals, plastics, electronics, and packaging of moisture-sensitive products.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Desiccant Dehumidification in Supermarkets
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Supermarkets often use desiccant dehumidification to avoid condensation on refrigerated casework.

evidence_text:
AS H R AE  G R E E N G U I D E   22 5
Desic c a n t Cool in g
a n d  D eh u m id ific a tion
GE N E RAL D E SCRI P TI O N
Ther e are two basic types of  open-cycle desiccant process:  solid  and
liq uid  desiccant.  Each of  these pr ocesses has several f orms,  and these
should be investigated to deter mine the most appropriate f or  the partic-
ular application.  B oth of  these systems need to have the air  being con-
ditioned  come in good contact with the d esiccant,  during which
moisture is absor bed  f rom the air and the temper atures of  both the air
and  d esiccant are coincid ently raised .
The moistur e absorption process is caused by desiccant having a
lower surf ace vapor pr essur e than the air.  As the temperature of  the
desiccant rises,  its vapor pressure r ises,  and its usef ul absorption capa-
bility lessens.  S ome systems,  particular ly liq uid  types,  have cooling of
air and desiccant coincident with dehumidif ication.
judge_reason: Evidence provided is about dynamic glazing, not supermarkets or desiccant dehumidification; evidence does not support claim.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Operation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Rotary desiccant dehumidifiers use solid desiccants like silica gel; humid process air is dehumidified in one part of the bed while a different part is dried for reuse by reactivation air.

evidence_text:
AS H R AE  G R E E N G U I D E   22 5

a n d  D eh u m id ific a tion

liq uid  desiccant.  Each of  these pr ocesses has several f orms,  and these
should be investigated to deter mine the most appropriate f or  the partic-
ular application.  B oth of  these systems need to have the air  being con-
ditioned  come in good contact with the d esiccant,  during which
moisture is absor bed  f rom the air and the temper atures of  both the air
and  d esiccant are coincid ently raised .
The moistur e absorption process is caused by desiccant having a
lower surf ace vapor pr essur e than the air.  As the temperature of  the
desiccant rises,  its vapor pressure r ises,  and its usef ul absorption capa-
bility lessens.  S ome systems,  particular ly liq uid  types,  have cooling of
air and desiccant coincident with dehumidif ication.
judge_reason: Rotary desiccant operation is generic background, but evidence is misattributed to dynamic glazing pages; no specific operational sequence supported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Desiccant Process Air Outlet Temperature
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The process air outlet temperature is higher than the inlet temperature primarily because the heat of sorption of the moisture removed is converted to sensible heat.

judge_reason: Process air outlet temperature explanation is generic description, not a measurable performance spec; lacks structured parameter values.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Solar Combi System Components
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A typical installation for the combined production of DHW and space heating (solar combi systems) includes solar collectors, a heat storage tank, and a boiler used as an auxiliary heater.

evidence_text:
